content.boundary: Applies to state-sector (Klebelsberg Centre-governed) 6/8-year gimnázium capacity and admission thresholds; does not extend to church, foundation, or private-maintained institutions, which fall outside state maintenance jurisdiction.
content.change_lever: Administrative tightening of score thresholds and formal capacity caps on state-maintained 6/8-year gimnázium places, using ministerial discretionary approval power already demonstrated in comparable cases.
content.name: Capacity-capped contraction of state-sector 6/8-year places via demographic-linked thresholds
content.system_problem: Extreme oversubscription (9-26x) combined with stagnant physical capacity and rising entrance-threshold recommendations indicates the selective track's absolute scale is currently sustained more by political and institutional inertia than by demonstrated capacity need, especially as birth cohorts shrink.
